note: kernelpatches is a CLI tool for orchestrating Photon kernel CVE backports. All findings flow from argparse-supplied --kernel-version / --output-dir / --photon-base into paths or URL/regex composition.

accepted.reason: ensure_photon_repo() opens an internal clone directory rooted at the CLI-supplied repo_base; CLI-by-design.
matrix.save_json target path under CLI-supplied output_dir.
Patch download path under CLI-supplied output_dir.
download_file target path under CLI-supplied output_dir.
Phase summary file under CLI-supplied output_dir.
CLI tool reads a CLI-supplied report path.
Tarball download path under CLI-supplied output_dir.
Extract destination under CLI-supplied output_dir; the *content* of the tar is now TarSlip-guarded.

download_file() target URL is built from get_kernel_org_url(self.kernel_version), which hardcodes https://cdn.kernel.org/pub/linux/kernel/vX.x/. The host is not attacker-controllable; only the path segment (kernel version) is, and that has been validated by argparse.

hard_fixes.fix: Wrapped tarfile.extractall in a TarSlip-guard loop: realpath each member against the extract root before extractall, raise RuntimeError on slip. Added 'import os'.
Same fix at L517 dominates the four other Snyk-flagged data-flow sinks; they were tainted-flow alerts pointing at the integrity-check and download call sites for the same downstream extract.
Replaced the bare tar.extract loop with a realpath-validated TarSlip guard before each tar.extract(member) call. Added 'import os'.
Wrapped kernel_version with re.escape() inside the fix_pattern f-string so CLI-supplied regex metacharacters cannot induce catastrophic backtracking.
Set csv.writer(..., quoting=csv.QUOTE_ALL) on the two writers in cve_matrix.py to defeat CSV-injection via cell content that starts with =, +, -, @.
